2026-07-10.prompt: You've been paired with one random player. Split or steal?
2026-07-10.sub: If you both split: 50 pts each. If you steal and they split: you take 100, they get 0. If you both steal: nothing for anyone.
2026-07-10.factoid: The mind game: stealing dominates on paper. But most days over half the crowd splits anyway.
2026-07-10.roast: 58% of players split today. The other 42% are why we can't have nice things.

2026-07-11.prompt: Players were asked: coffee or tea, forever. What % said COFFEE?
2026-07-11.sub: You're not answering the question — you're predicting the crowd's answer. Closest guesses to the true percentage win.
2026-07-11.factoid: The mind game: it's not what YOU would drink. It's what a few thousand strangers on the internet would drink.
2026-07-11.roast: 61% of today's players chose coffee for life. Tea loyalists overestimated their numbers, as tea loyalists do.

2026-07-12.prompt: Pick the topping the fewest players will pick.
2026-07-12.sub: The least popular topping today wins. Everyone is trying to be the odd one out — which is exactly why they'll fail.
2026-07-12.factoid: The mind game: the 'safe' choice is only safe until everyone agrees it's safe.
2026-07-12.roast: Only 9% picked Plain Cheese — it won. Everyone overthought pineapple and walked right into pepperoni.

2026-07-13.prompt: You've been paired with one random player. Split or steal?
2026-07-13.sub: If you both split: 50 pts each. If you steal and they split: you take 100, they get 0. If you both steal: nothing for anyone.
2026-07-13.factoid: The mind game: yesterday's splitters got burned by today's opportunists. Trends don't repeat, they say.
2026-07-13.roast: 64% split. The all-steal pairs walked away with nothing but shame.

2026-07-14.sub: The winner is whoever lands closest to two-thirds of the average of every number picked today. Think about what everyone else will think everyone else will pick…
2026-07-14.factoid: The mind game: if everyone picked randomly, the average would be 50 and the target 33. But everyone knows that… so they pick 33, making the target 22. But everyone knows THAT too…
2026-07-14.roast: The crowd averaged 41. The galaxy-brains who picked 0 got burned again.

2026-07-15.prompt: Pick the door the fewest players will pick.
2026-07-15.sub: The least popular door today wins. Everyone is trying to be the odd one out — which is exactly why they'll fail.
2026-07-15.factoid: The mind game: 'Plain looks boring, so no one picks it… but everyone thinks that, so everyone picks it.' Second-guessing yourself yet?
2026-07-15.roast: Only 5% picked the Plain door — it won. 33% convinced themselves Red was too obvious to be obvious.

2026-07-16.prompt: You've been paired with one random player. Split or steal?
2026-07-16.sub: If you both split: 50 pts each. If you steal and they split: you take 100, they get 0. If you both steal: nothing for anyone.
2026-07-16.factoid: The mind game: stealing dominates on paper. But paper doesn't have to live in the group chat afterward.
2026-07-16.roast: 61% of players split today. The 15% of steal-steal pairs got zero and deserve it.

2026-07-17.prompt: Players were asked: pizza or tacos, forever. What % said PIZZA?
2026-07-17.sub: You're not answering the question — you're predicting the crowd's answer. Closest guesses to the true percentage win.
2026-07-17.factoid: The mind game: it's not what YOU would eat. It's what a few thousand strangers on the internet would eat. Big difference.
2026-07-17.roast: 68% of today's players chose pizza for life. Taco loyalists overestimated their numbers, as taco loyalists do.

2026-07-18.sub: The winner is whoever lands closest to two-thirds of the average of every number picked today. Think about what everyone else will think everyone else will pick…
2026-07-18.factoid: The mind game: the smart move is to out-think the crowd's smartness by exactly one level. Not zero, not two.
2026-07-18.roast: The average landed at 36. The 0-pickers got clever and got crushed, same as every day.

2026-07-19.prompt: Pick the flavor the fewest players will pick.
2026-07-19.sub: The least popular flavor today wins. Everyone is trying to be the odd one out — which is exactly why they'll fail.
2026-07-19.factoid: The mind game: whatever you think is unpopular, a thousand other people think the exact same thing.
2026-07-19.roast: Pistachio's 9% took it. Vanilla players thought they were being ironic — 33% of them, apparently.
